name: Jake and Tess' Finding Monsters Adventure

reviews.review_description: r u going to update it for new vr generation, or stuck on this old version?
It tries to I stall and download fails.

reviews.review_title: Great visuals but makes no sense
reviews.review_description: I love the sound of this game but in practice it's just confusing and frustrating. The visuals are very nice, but the UI is full of dizzying occlusion violations (things visible on top of things they should be behind). Maybe you'll have more patience triggering each animation but I gave up after a couple of levels. Performance was very solid on my S7 at least.

reviews.review_title: Poor controls ruin potentially good game
reviews.review_description: I’ve played and reviewed every other title created by Black River Studios up to this point, and in those reviews I’ve written extensively about how much I respect and admire their craft. Most of all, I’ve been bewildered by the fact that their experiences were free, which bought complete goodwill regarding their titles’ flaws and limitations. I also loved how each experience was unique and tackled a different genre. So, naturally, when I came across this title and saw that it was an actual game and, of course, a different genre (kids/family adventure), I jumped at the chance. A few things to note about this game, especially if you’ve played other titles in their catalogue. First, it’s basically unplayable on the Go. It’s an early release of theirs, and seems to have been developed for the Gear VR. Chances are, it played fine then; it does not now. It does necessitate a gamepad; I’m using a Steelseries Stratus XL, which translates just fine with other games, but here it is not mapped out well at all. Perhaps it has to do with just this particular controller; I do not know for sure. What I do know is that I could barely control it at all, which was a huge disappointment. The irony is that this is the one app of theirs that carries a price tag, and it is the one that I cannot play. Which, really, is fine. After so many great VR experiences of theirs that I’ve had the pleasure of viewing for free, I don’t mind at all making what is essentially a donation. The sad part is just not being able to play it, as I’m sure it’s a great game that is a lot of fun based on their track record. I hate to give it a negative review, but I can’t lie to you and recommend something I can’t play. Maybe your controller will work better, maybe not. But at least you know you can still download Rock & Rails or Conflict0: Shattered for free and have a heck of a good time. Jake and Tess will just have to sit this one out.
